acquirers.name: RC BITE BID CO PTY LTD

targets.name: Yo My Goodness Pty Ltd as trustee for The Yo My Goodness IP Unit Trust

other_parties.name: THE YO MY COMPANY PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
River Capital Pty Ltd (**River Capital**), through a special purpose vehicle (RC Bite Bid Co Pty Ltd), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in YOMG OpCo (which will own the trading business operating under the “YOMG” brand), the Leaseholder Companies (which hold the leases for each YOMG location), and YOMG IP Co (which holds the YOMG Group's intellectual property) (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition will occur by way of a share acquisition, following a restructure of the YOMG Group.

River Capital is an Australian-incorporated private investment firm headquartered in Melbourne, Australia. River Capital manages a number of funds with varying strategies and investments in various portfolio companies.

The Cheesecake Shop, a River Capital portfolio company, is a retailer of cakes and desserts. It sells whole cakes and slices baked fresh on-site for take-home consumption, ordered in-store or online, as well as “dessert cups”. The Cheesecake Shop has approximately 255 stores across Australia and New Zealand.

The Yo My Company Pty Ltd and its associated entities operate food and beverage outlets in Australia under the “YOMG” brand. YOMG is a quick-service restaurant business supplying burgers, fries, wings, frozen yoghurt and related food and beverage products. YOMG currently operates 15 stores across Victoria and Queensland.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
River Capital Pty Ltd (River Capital) applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition, through a special purpose
vehicle (RC Bite Bid Co Pty Ltd), of 100% of the issued shares in
YOMG OpCo (which will own the trading business operating under
the “YOMG” brand), the Leaseholder Companies (which hold the
leases for each YOMG location), and YOMG IP Co (which holds the
YOMG Group's intellectual property), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, River Capital, is an Australian-incorporated private
investment firm headquartered in Melbourne, Australia. River Capital
manages a number of funds with varying strategies and investments
in various portfolio companies.
The Cheesecake Shop, a River Capital portfolio company, is a retailer
of cakes and desserts. It sells whole cakes and slices baked fresh
on-site for take-home consumption, ordered in-store or online, as
well as “dessert cups”. The Cheesecake Shop has approximately 255
stores across Australia and New Zealand.
The Yo My Company Pty Ltd and its associated entities operate food
and beverage outlets in Australia under the “YOMG” brand. YOMG is
a quick-service restaurant business supplying burgers, fries, wings,
frozen yoghurt and related food and beverage products. YOMG
currently operates 15 stores across Victoria and Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the retail supply of dessert
products in Victoria, Queensland and Australia is estimated
to be low and the increase in share arising from the
Acquisition would be small
b. there are alternative retail suppliers of dessert products near
YOMG stores in Victoria and Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
